originalTitle: 'This tree was planted by my ancestor hundreds of years ago and my family settled here'
title: A Super Old Tree and a Family's Big Secret in Ghana!
content:
Hello, Super Reporters! Get Ready for an Amazing Story!

Hello, my wonderful friends! Your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa is here! Do you know how much I love finding super cool stories? Well, today, I have a truly special one for you. It's about a magical tree and a family who have lived in the same place for a very, very long time. This story comes from a beautiful country in Africa called Ghana. It's like a secret kept by the wind and the leaves, passed down through generations.

Meet the Wonderful People of Ghana!

Ghana is a lovely country in West Africa. It has sunny beaches, yummy food, and very friendly people! Our story takes us to a special place in Ghana: a fishing town. What's a fishing town? Imagine a town right by the big, blue ocean, where people wake up early to catch fish in their boats. The air smells salty, and you can hear the waves crashing. It's a busy, lively place! Did you know? Ghana is famous for its delicious cocoa beans, which are used to make chocolate! Yum!

A Tree from a Long, Long Time Ago!

Now, let's talk about the star of our story: a very, very old tree! How old do you think it is? Ten years? Fifty years? No, much, much older! This tree was planted way back in the 13th Century. Wow! That's like saying it was planted over 700 years ago! Can you imagine? When this tree was just a little seedling, there were no cars, no televisions, and maybe even no schools like ours today! It's older than your great-great-great-great-grandparents! This tree has seen so many sunrises and sunsets. It has felt the gentle rain and the strong winds. It’s like a living, breathing history book standing tall in the middle of a busy town. It's probably the oldest living thing many people there have ever seen!

The Story of an Amazing Ancestor

So, who planted this amazing tree? It was planted by an ancestor of a family who lives there even today. What's an 'ancestor'? An ancestor is like a grandparent or great-grandparent who lived a very, very long time ago. So, one of this family's first members, many, many years ago, decided to plant a tree. Maybe it was to mark their home, or to give shade, or just because they loved nature. Imagine planting a tiny seed that grows into a giant tree that stands for 700 years! That's a super powerful act, isn't it? This ancestor started something that became a big part of their family's story forever.

Why Did the Family Settle Here?

Guess what? Because of this tree, the family decided to make this fishing town their home. The tree wasn't just a plant; it became a very important landmark. It was a place where they could gather, rest in its cool shade, and know they were home. It was like a big, green arrow pointing to 'Our Family's Beginning.' When people first settled in places a long time ago, they often looked for special spots. Maybe near water for drinking, or good land for farming, or a big, strong tree that could provide comfort and shelter. This family chose their home because of a tree that grew strong and tall, just like their family would.

Stories Told from Grandparents to Grandchildren

How do we know all this? This amazing story is not written in old books that are hundreds of years old. Instead, it's a story that is told! It’s called 'oral history.' This means that grandparents tell the story to their children, and then those children grow up and tell it to their children, and so on and so on. It's like a very special family secret that everyone in the family knows and shares. Each time someone tells the story, they remember their ancestor and the super old tree. It keeps the past alive and makes everyone feel connected. Did you know? In many parts of Africa, people share important stories and lessons through oral traditions like storytelling, songs, and poems! It's a beautiful way to learn!

Why is This Tree So Important?

This tree is more than just a big plant with leaves and branches. For this family, it's like a grandparent, a silent witness to everything that has happened in their family for centuries. It reminds them where they came from and how their family started. It's a symbol of their roots, just like the roots of the tree go deep into the earth. It shows how strong their family history is and how important it is to remember where you come from. Every time they look at the tree, they remember their ancestor and all the people who came before them.

Trees are Superheroes!

This story also reminds us how amazing trees are! Trees give us clean air to breathe, they give us shade when the sun is hot, and they are home to many birds and little creatures. They are like superheroes of nature, always helping us and our planet. But this tree in Ghana is an extra special superhero because it also holds a whole family's history in its leaves and branches. It tells a story without saying a single word! Did you know? Trees produce oxygen, which is the air we breathe! So, thank you, trees!

Let's Learn from the Past!

Stories like this one from Ghana teach us how important it is to listen to our elders and to know our own family history. They show us that things from a long, long time ago can still be important and teach us lessons today. This family's tree is a living lesson about family, history, and how a simple act can last for hundreds of years.

A Living Legend!

So, next time you see a big tree, remember this amazing story from Ghana. Remember the family whose history is tied to a tree planted by their ancestor over 700 years ago. It's a wonderful reminder that our world is full of incredible stories, and sometimes, the biggest stories are told by the oldest things around us. Let's cherish our trees and our family stories!
summary: In Ghana, a family's history in a fishing town is uniquely tied to a very old tree planted by their ancestor over 700 years ago, with their story passed down through generations by word of mouth.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows how old trees can connect families to their past, teach us about where we come from, and remind us that our history is all around us, even in nature.

title: US imposes sanctions on DR Congo ex-President Kabila alleging rebel support
content: The former leader has not responded to US accusations that he is aiming to destabilise the government.

originalTitle: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
title: Super Friends! China Helps Africa Trade Big!
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! Your friendly reporter is here with some super exciting news! Grab a snack and listen close, because something big is happening that can help our continent grow even stronger and happier.

What's All This About Extra Money?

Imagine you have a really cool toy that you made. Maybe it's a beautiful wooden animal or a colourful bead necklace. You want to sell it to someone in another town, very far away. Sometimes, when you send things to another country, their government asks for a little extra money. It's like a small fee for your toy to enter their country. We call this extra money a "tariff." Think of it like this: If your toy costs 10 coins, the other country might say, "Okay, but you have to pay us 2 extra coins for it to come here." So, your toy now costs 12 coins for the person buying it. This makes your toy more expensive.

China Says "No More Extra Money!"

Guess what? A very big country far away, called China, has just done something amazing for almost all of Africa! China has decided to say, "No more extra money!" for many, many things that come from Africa. This means that if our farmers grow delicious fruits or make cool crafts, and they want to send them to China, they won't have to pay that extra fee anymore. It's like China is saying, "Come on in, African goods, we welcome you!" Did you know? China is a super big country with lots and lots of people. There are more than 1.4 billion people living there! That's like so many of us here in Africa, all in one country!

Why is This Good News for Africa?

When there's no extra money to pay, things from Africa become cheaper for people in China to buy. And when things are cheaper, more people want to buy them! Imagine if your favourite juice was suddenly cheaper at the shop. You might buy more, right? It's the same idea. So, when more people in China buy things from Africa, it means our farmers can sell more of their fruits, our artists can sell more of their crafts, and our factories can sell more of the things they make.

More Sales Mean More Good Things!

When our African countries sell more things to China, it brings more money back to Africa. This money can help us in many ways. It can help our farmers buy better tools, or grow more crops. It can help our artists buy more materials to make even more beautiful things. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with 54 different countries! Each country has its own special things to share with the world, like tasty coffee, amazing textiles, and beautiful minerals. More sales can mean more jobs for people. When a farm needs to grow more food, they need more people to help plant and harvest. When a factory needs to make more clothes, they need more people to sew. More jobs mean more families have money to buy food, send their kids to school, and build better homes. This makes our communities stronger and happier!

Why is China Doing This?

You might be wondering, "Why is China being so kind?" Well, China wants to be a good friend to Africa. When a country helps another country in a friendly way, it's like they are showing how much they care. This helps them build strong friendships. We call this "soft power." It means being friendly and helpful, instead of just making deals or being bossy. China wants to show Africa that it's a good partner and friend. Did you know? China and many African countries have been friends and traded with each other for a very long time! This new rule makes their friendship even stronger.

What Are We Sending to China?

Africa makes so many wonderful things! We can send fresh fruits like mangoes and avocados, delicious coffee beans, and special nuts. We also have beautiful handmade crafts, like carvings and woven baskets. Our factories make clothes, shoes, and other useful items. And don't forget about the important natural resources like metals and minerals that China needs to make their own products.

Will Everyone Benefit the Same?

Now, here's something smart people who study countries and trade (we call them "analysts") say we should think about. Even though this is great news, it might not help every single African country in the exact same way. Imagine a big playground with lots of swings and slides. Everyone can play, but maybe some kids like the swings more, and some like the slides more. Some African countries might already have lots of things that China wants to buy. So, they might start selling a lot more, very quickly, and make more money. Other countries might not have as many things that China needs right now, or they might need more time to get ready to sell a lot. So, they might not see as much change right away. This is what "uneven gains" means. It means not everyone gets exactly the same amount of good stuff, or at the same speed. But it's still a big step forward for everyone. Did you know? Trade is like sharing! When countries trade, they share the things they are good at making or growing with other countries. This makes everyone's lives better.

What Does This Mean for You, Kids?

So, what does all this mean for you, our amazing future leaders of Africa? It means that as our countries get more money from selling things, our governments might have more money to spend on important things for you! This could mean better schools with more books and computers. It could mean better hospitals to help you when you're sick. It could mean safer roads and cleaner water. It could also mean that you might see more different kinds of toys or clothes from China in your shops, because it's easier and cheaper for them to come here too. Most importantly, it means Africa is building stronger friendships and making more money, which helps us all have a brighter future!

Let's Celebrate Friendship!

This is a big step for friendship between Africa and China. It shows that countries can work together to help each other grow and become stronger. It's like when you share your toys with a friend; everyone has more fun! When countries share trade opportunities, everyone benefits. So, let's cheer for more trade, more jobs, and more friendship across the world! We are growing, Africa!
summary: China is making it easier and cheaper for almost all African countries to sell their products to China, which will help Africa grow stronger.
whyMatters: This is important because when African countries sell more things, they earn more money. This money can help build better schools, hospitals, and create more jobs for people in Africa. It's like a big friendly hand to help Africa become even stronger and happier!

title: Livingston relegated after one season in Premiership
